**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 1: Cut-away drawing of the combustor:**
    *   Components:
        *   Preheated seeding air
        *   Metal side wall
        *   Radial swirler
        *   Fuel
        *   Quartz glass window
        *   Exhaust
        *   Exhaust gas transition duct
        *   Preheated air
*   **Fig. 2: Experimental setup for the imaging of the OH PLIF and the OH\* chemiluminescence:**
    * Components:
        * concave cylindrical lens
        * convex lens
        * aperture
        * dye cell
        * filter
        * beam splitter
        * ICCD camera
        * video camera
        * photo multiplier
        * beam splitter
        * laser system
        * matrix burner
        * combustor
        * ICCD filter camera

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Thermal power of combustor: 1 MW
*   Air preheating temperature: 400°C
*   Particle diameter for seeding: 1 µm
*   Seeded air mass flow: up to 4 g/s
*   Pressure range (p): 1.5 bars to 6 bars
*   Burner pressure drops (Δp): 1% to 3% (percentage refers to absolute pressure upstream)
*   Dye laser wavelength for OH PLIF: 283.55 nm.
*   OH radical band: A-X (1-0), Q₁(8) line.
*   Spectral bandpass filter: Transparent for wavelengths between 300 nm and 325 nm
*   Lens distances: Lenses are set up with a distance of about 64 mm from each other
*   Lens focal lengths: f=-50 mm (cylindrical), f=750 mm (spherical)

title: 2010_flow_field_and_combustion_characteristics_of_premixed_gas_combustion_by_PLI
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the technical information extracted from the document:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   f1 = -12.7 mm, f2 = 200 mm
*   f = 1000 mm
* λ = 532 nm

**2. Graphs/Charts:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Technical details of the PLIF and chemiluminescence diagnostics**

    *   **OH\* chemiluminescence measurement**
        *   Exposure time: 40 μm
        *   Frame rate: 3 Hz
        *   Effective image size: 512x446 pixels
        *   Bandpass filter: 300-325 nm
    *   **OH PLIF measurement**
        *   Exposure time: 400 ns
        *   Laser pulse duration: 8 ns
        *   Laser pulse energy: 4.6-5.5 mJ
        *   Laser wavelength: 283.55 nm
        *   Effective image size: 359x446 pixels
        *   Bandpass filter: 300-325 nm
    *   **ICCD camera specifications (both cameras)**
        *   Dynamic range: 16 bits
        *   CCD chip size: 512x512 pixels
        *   Objective (flame): UV Halle, 100 mm, f/2
        *   Objective (dye cell): UV Nikkor, 105 mm, f/4.5
*   **Table 2: Technical details of the PIV setup**

    *   **PIV measurement**
        *   Laser pulse duration: 5 ns
        *   Laser pulse energy: 120 mJ
        *   Laser sheet width: 1 mm
        *   Double-pulse delay: 10-15 μs
        *   PIV repetition rate: 5 Hz
        *   Seeding material: TiO₂ (1 μm)
        *   PIV camera objective: Nikkor, 50 mm, f/5.6

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 3: Experimental setup for the PIV.**
    *   Components and labels include: beam expansion (cylindrical lenses), convex lens, aperture, ICCD camera, PIV laser, video camera, filter (300-325 nm), filter (532 nm), combustor, CCD camera for PIV

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Temperature in combustion chamber: >1600°C
*   Temperature of window surface: Up to 1000°C
*   Interrogation window size (PIV): 12x12 pixels
*   PIV velocity accuracy: ±0.7 m/s
*   Subpixel accuracy: 0.1 pixel

title: 2010_flow_field_and_combustion_characteristics_of_premixed_gas_combustion_by_PLI
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
*   No explicit equations, but the text refers to: `Ap=1%` and `Φ=0.59` and the value `Φ=0.55`

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 4:**
    *   Axes: Not explicitly labeled; represents planar flow velocity.
    *   Units: Planar flow velocity (color coded, qualitative), shown as a percentage 0% to max.
    *   Curves: Streamlines showing flow direction.
    *   Key Values: Measurements at p=3 bars, Ap=1%, and Φ=0.59. PIV repetition rate was 5 Hz

*   **Figure 5:**
    *   Axes: Not explicitly labeled; x,y represents spatial location.
    *   Units: OH* chemiluminescence intensity (color coded, qualitative), shown as a percentage 0% to max.
    *   Curves: Represents OH* chemiluminescence intensity distribution.
    *   Key Values: p=4 bars, Ap=1%, and Φ=0.55

**3. Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 4(a), (b), (c):**
    *   Components: Planar flow velocity fields represented as streamlines.
    *   Labels: Arrows indicate local flow direction. Burner nozzle indicated by marks on the left side of the image.

*   **Figure 5:**
    *   Components: OH* chemiluminescence distribution.
    *   Labels: "OH* chemiluminescence intensity", White ellipse representing burner nozzle exit.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   p = 3 bars (PIV measurements)
*   p = 4 bars (OH chemiluminescence)
*   Δp = 1%
*   Φ = 0.59 (PIV measurements)
*   Φ = 0.55 (OH chemiluminescence measurement)
*   PIV repetition rate = 5 Hz
*   Frame rate (for averaged images) = 3-5 Hz
*   Vortex size: 1cm to 5cm
*   Region of heat release: between 5cm and 8 cm from burner exit.
*   Averaged image based on 200 single shots
*   Background images an average of 200 shots

title: 2010_flow_field_and_combustion_characteristics_of_premixed_gas_combustion_by_PLI
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided research page:

**1. Mathematical equations:**

*   Δp=1.1% at p=1.5 bars and Φ =0.53
*   p=4 bars, Δp=3%, and Φ=0.50
*   p=4, Δp=1%, and Φ=0.53
*   p=4 bars, Δp =1%, and Φ=0.56

**2. Graphs/charts:**

*   **Figure 6 OH PLIF intensity mapping:**
    *   Y-axis: OH PLIF intensity
    *   Units: %, scaled 0 to max.
    *   Curves: OH radical distribution in flames
    *   Key values:
        *   (a) p=1.5 bars, Δp =1.1%, and Φ=0.53
        *   (b) p=4 bars, Δp=3%, and Φ=0.50
*   **Figure 7 Absolute gradient of OH PLIF intensity:**
    *   Y-axis: absolute gradient of OH PLIF intensity
    *   Units: %, scaled -50 to max.
    *   Curves: Flame zone edge visualization
    *   Key values: p=4 bars

*   **Figure 8 Mean Flow Field:**
    *   Description: vector field overlaid on concentration contours
    *   Curves: Contour plot of corresponding mean OH density
    *   Vectors represent the mean flow field direction and magnitude
    *   Key values: p=4 bars, Δp =1%, and Φ=0.56

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   Temperature: 1400 K (approximate temperature above which OH is present in the hot exhaust gas)
*   Time: 0.33 s (interval between successive PLIF images), few milliseconds (relaxation toward equilibrium at p=1 bar)
*   Pressure: p = 1 bar, 1.5 bars, 4 bars
*   Δp: 1.1%, 3%, 1%
*   Φ: 0.53, 0.50, 0.56
